verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is an EGF-family ligand synthesized as a single-pass type I transmembrane precursor (pro-epiregulin) anchored in the plasma membrane before regulated ectodomain shedding by ADAM proteases releases the soluble mature form. The membrane-anchored precursor is the canonical biosynthetic form and supports juxtacrine signaling at the cell surface before cleavage. However, the TM precursor stage is transient — the dominant biological actor is the shed soluble ectodomain that binds EGFR and ERBB4. This architecture fits the 'transient TM precursor of a shed-ligand-dominant gene' pattern: `contextual` / `dual_localization`. The surface precursor is real and targetable but not the stable canonical surface form; most clinical antibody programs target the soluble pool.
